description:
## Parent

Parent epic: `bsm-dbh` — Filtering and search issue list views.

## Background

Unit and component tests can prove each layer in isolation, but this feature crosses the real desktop stack: disposable Beadwork workspace, Rust CLI adapter, TauRPC, Effect service, React sidebar, Issue List, search, and Issue Detail. The project already has an Issue List WebDriver suite that launches the built desktop binary. That suite should gain one focused happy-path scenario for this feature without becoming an exhaustive matrix of every view/search combination.

The existing WebDriver documentation currently lists search/filter coverage as out of scope; that documentation must change when this slice ships.

## What to build

Add or update one focused WebDriver end-to-end scenario proving the real desktop binary supports sidebar Issue List View selection and local Issue Search. The fixture workspace should include enough Beadwork data to prove distinct views:

- At least one Ready Issue.
- At least one Blocked Issue with an actual blocker dependency relationship.
- At least one Closed Issue.
- At least one Deferred Issue.

The scenario should verify representative behavior such as:

- The combined load renders the sidebar counts and current workspace.
- Selecting a sidebar view changes the visible rows.
- Searching narrows the active view locally.
- Selecting a visible Issue still renders Issue Detail content through the real app path.

Update `docs/agents/webdriver-e2e.md` or the relevant suite docs to explain the new search/view coverage and remove the stale claim that search/filter coverage is out of scope.

## Technical Approach

Extend the existing disposable fixture helper using real `bw` commands. Prefer deterministic titles/descriptions so WebDriver assertions can be clear. Use `bw dep add <blocker> blocks <blocked>` to create a genuinely blocked Issue rather than faking dependency fields. Use `bw close` and `bw defer`/creation flags as needed so closed and deferred views are real Beadwork states.

Keep the scenario small. It should prove the integrated path, not duplicate all component tests. Include clear logging so failures identify fixture setup, RPC/load, sidebar interaction, search interaction, or detail rendering.

## Considerations

- The e2e scenario should use the real built desktop binary, not a renderer-only test or mocked command result.
- Avoid brittle assertions on exact counts unless the fixture fully controls them.
- If the TauRPC command name changes from `list_issues` to `load_issue_explorer_data` for this path, update direct sanity checks accordingly while keeping any backward-compatible command tests intentional.
- Preserve existing empty and success scenarios unless intentionally replaced by better coverage.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The WebDriver fixture includes ready, blocked-with-real-blocker, closed, and deferred Issues.
- [ ] The WebDriver scenario proves sidebar view selection changes visible rows in the real desktop app.
- [ ] The scenario proves local search narrows the active view.
- [ ] The scenario still proves representative Issue Detail rendering after selecting a visible row.
- [ ] WebDriver docs describe the new view/search coverage and no longer list search/filter coverage as wholly out of scope.
- [ ] The scenario has useful logging for fixture setup and app interaction failures.

## Test Plan

- Run the existing e2e build and issue-list suite locally.
- Verify the new scenario fails clearly if `bw` is missing, fixture creation fails, the app cannot load issue views, sidebar selection fails, or search does not narrow rows.
- Keep unit/component coverage as the exhaustive behavior source; e2e remains one integrated smoke/happy path.
title: Prove Issue List View selection and search in desktop WebDriver
